subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Uranus: The Sideways Planet
description: A 60-second lesson on Uranus: The Sideways Planet.

hook.text: Uranus spins on its side, tilted 98 degrees. Each pole faces the Sun for 42 years, then darkness for 42.

buildup.text: It's an ice giant—mostly water, methane, and ammonia ices around a small rocky core.

discovery.text: Methane absorbs red light, giving Uranus its pale blue-green color. It's a frigid −224°C.

twist.text: Something enormous hit Uranus long ago, knocking it sideways. Likely an Earth-sized impactor.

climax.text: Voyager 2 is the only spacecraft to visit Uranus, flying past in 1986. It remains barely explored.

punchline.text: Knocked sideways by a cosmic impact. Still spinning alone.

quiz.question: Why does Uranus spin on its side?
options: A massive collision with an Earth-sized object tilted it long ago
The gravity of Neptune gradually pulled it sideways
It formed that way from the original solar nebula
